factors.name: US-Iran Geopolitical Escalation
factors.summary: Fresh US strikes on Iran for second straight day, Iran retaliatory missile attacks on Bahrain/Kuwait/Qatar, ceasefire collapsed. Oil surged 7% to $74.50+, raising inflation risk and energy cost concerns for equities.

factors.name: Oil & Energy Cost Inflation Risk
factors.summary: WTI extended gains above $74 on Strait of Hormuz disruption fears and Trump's threat to target Kharg Island. Higher energy costs compress tech margins and risk Fed hold/hike bias if inflation re-accelerates.

factors.name: China CPI Disappointment
factors.summary: China CPI fell to 1.0% YoY in June vs 1.1% expected; PPI surged to 4.1% 4-year high. Two-track economy (weak domestic demand, strong export pricing) signals demand headwinds for tech hardware and cloud infrastructure.

factors.name: Chipmaker Strength & AI Optimism
factors.summary: SK Hynix US listing 7x+ oversubscribed; Broadcom-Apple supply deal and reports of limited Nvidia H200 China approval lifted semiconductor sentiment. Nvidia BofA note argues valuation anomaly at 11-year PE low vs peers.

factors.name: Real Yields & Duration Risk
factors.summary: Gold dropped to $4,075 amid oil spike and renewed inflation/rate-hike fears from Trump's Iran escalation rhetoric. Long-duration growth multiples vulnerable if nominal rates re-anchor higher.

factors.name: Fed Messaging (FOMC Minutes Context)
factors.summary: FOMC minutes noted 'a few' participants saw case for rate raises. Combined with oil inflation risk, this tilts toward hawkish hold bias; no imminent cut expectations.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings Calendar
factors.summary: No mega-cap earnings announced in this window. Next catalysts likely Apple, Microsoft, Nvidia in coming weeks; earnings season is main NDX alpha driver.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Valuation
factors.summary: Oil surge and geopolitical risk typically support USD near-term; strong dollar headwind for tech multinationals' foreign revenue and multiple expansion.

factors.name: Sentiment & Positioning
factors.summary: Bitcoin tumbled to $62K support; crypto weakness often signals risk-off rotation. Retail SPX/NDX enthusiasm was crowded long on USD strength; geopolitical shock may flush complacency.

factors.name: Price Action Context (4h/24h)
factors.summary: NAS100 up only +0.12% in 4h and +0.60% in 24h despite oil+7%, Iran war, China miss. Weak breadth suggests rally is stalling; move not extended enough to confirm phase-3 but lack of conviction is red flag.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught in a tug-of-war between chipmaker/AI strength (SK Hynix IPO hype, Broadcom-Apple deal) and escalating macro headwinds (US-Iran war restart, oil +7%, China growth miss, real yield re-acceleration). The index is up only 0.6% over 24h despite headline risk, signaling weak conviction and likely consolidation or pullback near resistance. Geopolitical shock could unmask crowded long positioning if oil holds above $74 and triggers inflation repricing.
catalysts_ahead: US Initial Jobless Claims (Thursday)
Oil supply disruption updates from Strait of Hormuz / Trump Iran rhetoric
China stimulus response or fiscal data (likely weak given June CPI miss)
FOMC participants' hawkish commentary on rate hikes
Mega-cap earnings guidance over next 2-3 weeks (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL)
Real yields & 10Y Treasury move if oil/inflation premium persists
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if (1) Iran-US ceasefire suddenly restored and oil crashes below $72, (2) Fed explicitly signals cut cycle ahead, (3) chipmaker/hyperscaler earnings surprise to upside with strong forward guidance, (4) China announces major fiscal stimulus (rare given local-govt austerity). Conversely, further oil rally past $76 and/or Trump targeting Kharg Island would confirm BEARISH and trigger sharp selloff in duration longs.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in progress but weak breadth (flat 24h price vs. headline risk) suggests no conviction yet. Wait for retest of 29,050–29,150 support before considering short; alternatively, wait for clarity on Iran ceasefire status and oil hold above/below $74 before adding long. Avoid chasing highs into earnings season with macro this fragmented.

factors.name: US-Iran Escalation / Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: Fresh US strikes on Iran overnight, Iranian retaliatory ballistic missiles on Bahrain/Kuwait/Jordan bases, Strait of Hormuz traffic near-halted. This is a genuine supply disruption risk that historically weighs on growth equities and compressed multiples.

factors.name: Oil Price Spike / Energy Cost Inflation
factors.summary: WTI holding near $74-75.75, Goldman warns Hormuz flare-up may delay oil supply recovery. Rising energy costs feed into inflation narrative and squeeze profit margins for tech/growth names with thin real yields.

factors.name: China Economic Data Mixed / Deflation vs Producer Inflation
factors.summary: China June CPI came in at 1.0% YoY (vs 1.2% expected, prior 1.2%), but PPI spiked to 4.1% YoY (4-year high). Weak consumer demand + rising input costs = margin squeeze in China, dampening semiconductor and capex demand signals.

factors.name: Fed Policy Uncertainty / Real Yields Pivot
factors.summary: Fed Meeting Minutes Wednesday sparked USD weakness as markets repriced rate expectations lower. AI inflation fears offset dovish signals per Fed policymakers. Longer-duration NDX names sensitive to real yield moves; uncertainty persists on peak rates.

factors.name: Semiconductor / AI Capex Sentiment
factors.summary: SK Hynix US ADR $28B listing heavily oversubscribed, Broadcom-Apple supply deal, Nvidia $500M Australian cloud investment, reports China may allow limited Nvidia H200 sales. Chip demand constructive despite macro headwinds.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness vs DXY
factors.summary: USD/DXY near 101.00, pressured for second straight day on Fed repricing and Iran risk. Weaker dollar typically headwind for US equities with heavy international revenue; also reduces real yield pressure temporarily but adds FX volatility.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings / Forward Guidance Void
factors.summary: No major earnings or hyperscaler capex guidance in the news window. Levi's Q2 beat but guided weak, other mega-caps silent. Absence of bullish catalyst limits upside conviction.

factors.name: Macro Volatility / Market Consolidation
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.12% 4h, +0.60% 24h—minimal directional progress despite headline volatility. Market consolidating after recent Trump de-escalation bounce. Lack of conviction breakout amid geopolitical and policy uncertainty.

factors.name: Bank of Japan Rate Hike Signal / Yen Intervention Risk
factors.summary: BoJ officials warning of potential rate hikes above 2%, USD/JPY near 162.00s (4-decade high). Yen strength could trigger intervention, add volatility to risk assets and complicate Fed/growth narrative.

factors.name: VIX / Risk Asset Positioning
factors.summary: Overnight volatility from Iran escalation present, but not reflected in extreme positioning data in news. Oil, crypto, and equities holding steadier than expected; risk-off not yet severe but lingering caution evident.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is consolidating after Trump's initial de-escalation bounce, now facing renewed US-Iran military clashes that threaten oil supply and feed inflation fears. Fed policy uncertainty (dovish repricing after Wednesday minutes) clashes with AI capex upside and geopolitical downside—a classic PHASE_2 tug-of-war. Semiconductor demand signals remain constructive (SK Hynix listing, Broadcom-Apple, China H200), but China's weak CPI (1.0% vs 1.2%) and soaring PPI (4.1%) signal margin pressure and deflationary domestic demand. The real yield picture is uncertain: weaker dollar and Fed repricing support growth equities, but rising oil and BoJ hike signals reintroduce real yield headwinds. Markets are pricing caution into the close of trading.
catalysts_ahead: US weekly Initial Jobless Claims (Thursday AM US time)
Germany Trade Balance (Thursday European session)
Bank of Korea rate decision next Thursday (hike now widely expected)
Fed speakers / further Powell/Barkin commentary on AI inflation
Iran developments and Strait of Hormuz shipping updates (real-time)
Mega-cap earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN) in coming weeks
FOMC minutes, PCE inflation data (critical for duration risk)
China PBoC policy response to weak CPI
screening.risk_warning: If Iran-US escalation contains quickly and oil rolls over below $72, or if Fed signals faster rate cuts, NAS100 could rally 1-2% into resistance. Conversely, if Hormuz traffic remains disrupted, oil spikes above $78, or geopolitical risk premia persist, consolidation breaks lower to 29000–28950. Earnings surprises from mega-caps (especially NVDA, MSFT cloud guidance) would override macro backdrop; absence of such catalysts leaves bias vulnerable to mean-reversion chop.
screening.entry_guidance: Consolidation-bound; avoid chasing into resistance at 29350–29400. Consider nibbling long breakout above 29400 only if oil stabilizes sub-$74 and Iran tensions fade. For shorts, wait for retest of 29200–29250 with confirmed rejection of 29350 resistance before scaling in on risk-off acceleration.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran)
factors.summary: Second day of US strikes on Iran, but Trump signaled de-escalation ('Iran wants to make a deal'). Risk premium elevated but resolution narrative emerging; market has largely repriced initial shock.

factors.name: Oil & Energy Shock
factors.summary: WTI slipped below $74 after recent rally; Strait of Hormuz transits dropping per vessel tracking. Oil volatility supports Fed hawk positioning but elevated energy costs pose Q3 margin risk to tech.

factors.name: Real Yields & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: ECB hawkish repricing (three hikes by year-end per Deutsche Bank); Fed hawks supported by energy spike and 2Y yields rising. Long-duration growth equities (NQ's core holding) are yield-sensitive and facing headwinds.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings
factors.summary: PepsiCo missed; AstraZeneca crashed 9% on failed heart drug trial. No major NDX earnings catalysts in this window yet, but consumer weakness signal (PepsiCo budget tightening) hints at macro softening.

factors.name: AI Capex Narrative
factors.summary: BlackRock's Jewell noted AI capex sustainable for 2–3 years; SK Hynix Nasdaq listing (2nd largest ever) underway; SpaceX/AI wealth fueling luxury demand. Hyperscaler order trends remain constructive.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD Index near 101, supported by energy shock and Fed hawk repricing. Stronger dollar headwind for NQ's international revenue exposure (~40% for mega-caps).

factors.name: Positioning & Volatility
factors.summary: Nasdaq futures advanced 0.74% overnight (per FXStreet 8:50 AM) but spot NQ +0.12% in 4h suggests rangebound chop. No gamma extremes or dealer skew signals detected in headlines.

factors.name: CPI / Inflation Data Ahead
factors.summary: No CPI print in this window mentioned; PCE and core inflation data expected soon. Markets await inflation surprise; if hot, will accelerate Fed tightening and compress multiples further.

factors.name: Sector Rotation & Downgrades
factors.summary: Wolfe Research downgraded multiple financials/insurers (Ryan Specialty, Gallagher, Equitable, Tractor Supply by Mizuho). Breadth concerns and rotation away from momentum names.

factors.name: South Korea KOSPI Contagion
factors.summary: KOSPI entered bear market (20% below June high) on hawkish BOJ backdrop and overstretched positioning. SK Hynix listing and weak domestic sentiment could weigh on semiconductor optimism.

screening.narrative: NAS100 remains stuck in a rangebound consolidation after the initial shock from renewed US–Iran tensions. Trump's de-escalation signal has relieved immediate war premium, but underlying headwinds persist: ECB/Fed hawkish repricing on energy shock, elevated real yields crushing long-duration growth multiples, and mixed consumer/earnings backdrop (PepsiCo miss, AstraZeneca collapse). AI capex narrative provides some support, but geopolitical fragility, dollar strength, and deteriorating breadth (KOSPI bear market, sector-wide downgrades) limit upside conviction. Market is pricing consolidation ahead of CPI/FOMC clarity.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI release (likely this week) – hot print could re-accelerate Fed tightening
FOMC decision (next ~2 weeks) – guidance on rate path critical for duration multiples
NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL earnings (next 2–3 weeks) – hyperscaler capex guidance essential
SK Hynix IPO completion – barometer for semiconductor demand narrative
ECB September hike odds (repricing ongoing) – impact on USD carry and EM unwinding
Further Middle East escalation or ceasefire confirmation – oil trajectory and risk-on/off regime
screening.risk_warning: If CPI prints hot (>3.5% YoY), Fed tightening accelerates, and real yields spike further, NQ could drop 2–3% as growth multiples compress. Conversely, if Iran–US ceasefire holds and oil falls, geopolitical premium reverses and tech could rally into earnings. Hawkish BOJ (rate hikes above 2%) could trigger USD/JPY correction and unwind carry trades, causing sudden volatility. SK Hynix supply overhang or semiconductor order weakness would invalidate AI capex optimism.
screening.entry_guidance: Range-bound consolidation; avoid chasing. Consider nibbling longs on dips to 29100–29150 support with tight stops below 29000. Resistance at 29400–29500 faces headwind from real yield elevation; wait for Fed clarity or CPI print before committing to directional conviction.

factors.name: US-Iran Geopolitical Escalation
factors.summary: Trump declared ceasefire with Iran over; Iran rushed 11M barrels of crude exports in 24h; Strait of Hormuz transit nearing standstill. Risk-off sentiment pressuring growth equities, but move already partially priced into commodity complex.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Stance (June Minutes)
factors.summary: Fed's Williams reaffirmed inflation risks remain on upside; June FOMC minutes showed hawkish hold with split on rate outlook. ECB accounts also flagged upside inflation risks. Hawkish bias pressures long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Labor Market Resilience
factors.summary: US jobless claims came in at 215K vs 218K estimate; 4-week average 218.75K. Layoffs remain limited, supporting consumer spending backdrop for earnings growth.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings Weakness
factors.summary: PepsiCo missed estimates on North America weakness (consumer budget-tightening); AstraZeneca dropped 9% after heart drug trial failure. Magnificent Seven notably absent from 2026 rally per Bloomberg. Single-name weakness in consumer/healthcare spreading sentiment concerns.

factors.name: Semiconductor & AI Capex Strength
factors.summary: Micron committed $250B to US expansion through 2035; Broadcom/Anthropic $35B AI chip credit deal entering trading; SK Hynix pricing US listing at $149/ADR. Hyperscaler infrastructure build-out remains robust despite macro headwinds.

factors.name: Real Yields (TIPS) & Dollar Dynamics
factors.summary: Dollar mixed intraday (lower on Iran deal optimism, recovering on safe-haven bid post-escalation). Real yields under pressure from upside inflation risks but supported by Fed hawkishness. Conflicting signals limit conviction on duration.

factors.name: Oil Price Surge & Supply Risk
factors.summary: Brent oil supported by Hormuz transit disruption fears; supply recovery from US-Iran MoU temporarily halted. Energy sector repricing, but broader equity sentiment torn between supply shock (inflationary) and risk-off (growth negative).

factors.name: Analyst Sentiment Shift
factors.summary: Charles Schwab strategists warned era of easy index gains is over; KeyBanc downgraded Salesforce citing AI narrative doubts. Narrative shifting from synchronized AI boom to selectivity and earnings quality concerns.

factors.name: VIX/Risk-Off Flow Indicators
factors.summary: BTC ETF flows flipped negative per CoinDesk morning brief. Emerging markets extended declines; safe-haven flows into USD and Treasuries on geopolitical escalation. Risk-off positioning taking hold.

factors.name: Upcoming Catalysts (Within 72h)
factors.summary: No imminent FOMC, CPI, or major tech earnings disclosed in 24h window. Market likely to remain range-bound and reactive to Iran-US headlines until next significant economic data or earnings calendar.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between two conflicting narratives: (1) Hawkish Fed, upside inflation risks, and geopolitical escalation (Iran-US tensions, Hormuz disruption) are pressuring long-duration growth equities and triggering risk-off flows; (2) Robust AI/semiconductor capex commitments (Micron $250B, Broadcom $35B), labor market resilience, and selective tech strength (chip stocks rallying, SK Hynix IPO) provide a floor. The Magnificent Seven's recent underperformance relative to the broader market and analyst sentiment downgrades (Schwab, KeyBanc) suggest conviction in the mega-cap narrative is fracturing. Macro headwinds (inflation stickiness, geopolitical tail risk) and earnings misses (PepsiCo, AstraZeneca) are offsetting positive semicon/AI infrastructure commentary. No fresh major catalyst in the 24h window; market remains range-bound and reactive to Middle East headlines.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran ceasefire stability or escalation (next 48h critical)
Oil price reaction to Hormuz transit disruption (real-time)
CPI or PCE inflation print (likely next week)
FOMC meeting (timing unclear in news, but hawkish bias already priced)
Next major tech earnings (Apple, Microsoft, Google likely within 2-3 weeks)
Treasury 10Y yield reaction to geopolitical premium unwinding
Semiconductor order guidance from hyperscalers (cloud capex commentary)
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran de-escalates quickly (Trump says Iran wants deal), risk-on rally could resume and push NAS100 higher as duration pressure eases. Conversely, if Hormuz transit fully closes or major supply shock emerges, oil spike could drive lasting inflation concerns, forcing aggressive Fed stance and triggering 2-3% drawdown in equities. Earnings misses from mega-caps (PepsiCo miss, AstraZeneca trial failure) could cascade into broader guidance cuts, invalidating 'earnings growth' bullish case. Real yields remaining sticky at elevated levels would continue to pressure tech multiples regardless of near-term geopolitical outcomes.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway (risk-off, geopolitical shock priced in). Avoid chasing lower; wait for retest of support near 20,250 or stabilization in oil prices and VIX before considering fresh long entries. If escalation de-escalates and dollar rolls over, potential mean-reversion long setup near 20,300–20,350. For shorts, wait for breakdown below 20,200 with volume confirmation before adding.
